ogTitle: Taylors Flat vs Tea Gardens: Climate Safety

heading: Taylors Flat vs Tea Gardens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Taylors Flat, New South Wales and Tea Gardens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Taylors Flat has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Tea Gardens (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Taylors Flat or Tea Gardens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Taylors Flat has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Tea Gardens scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Taylors Flat scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Tea Gardens scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Taylors Flat or Tea Gardens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Taylors Flat has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
